metadata.sourceOfferedTerms:
Spring
The course is offered in odd-numbered years in the spring semester
metadata.recommendedPrerequisiteText: Basic knowledge in molecular biology and DNA analyses. Basic knowledge in R and some insight in programming/scripting will be highly advantageous.

Fundamental knowledge of ordinary differential equations and their numerical solution, corresponding to IN1900 – Introduction to Programming with Scientific Applications and MAT-INF1100 – Modelling and Computations (discontinued) or equivalent. It is also useful to have a basic knowledge of partial differential equations, corresponding to the introductory parts of MAT3360 – Introduction to Partial Differential Equations , or similar.
Formal coding experience is important and used heavily during the course. We offer an introductory Python workshop the Sunday evening before the course starts, and any student without substantial Python experience should expect to take that workshop.

This course is open to students studying biology, mathematics or computer science. Students must have at least 120 study points to apply. Students must have a basic understanding of biology/molecular biology at least corresponding to 10 university study points in molecular biology or similar.
The course might be relevant also for students without this background, but candidates should in this case contact the course coordinator before signing up.

Fundamental knowledge of ordinary differential equations and their numerical solution, corresponding to IN1900 – Introduction to Programming with Scientific Applications MAT-INF1100 – Modelling and Computations (discontinued) or equivalent. It is also useful to have a basic knowledge of partial differential equations, corresponding to the introductory parts of MAT3360 – Introduction to Partial Differential Equations or similar.
Formal coding experience is important and used heavily during the course. We offer an introductory Python workshop the Sunday evening before the course starts, and any student without substantial Python experience should expect to take that workshop.

This course is open to students studying biology, mathematics or computer science. Students must have at least 120 study points to apply. Students must have a basic understanding of biology/molecular biology at least corresponding to 10 university study points in molecular biology or similar.
The course might be relevant also for students without this background, but candidates should in this case contact the course coordinator before signing up.

title: Genetikk og utviklingsbiologi
prerequisiteText:
I tillegg til generell studiekompetanse eller realkompetanse må du dekke spesielle opptakskrav.
Du må ha:
Matematikk R1 (eller Matematikk S1 og S2) + R2
Og en av disse:
Fysikk (1+2)
Kjemi (1+2)
Biologi (1+2)
Informasjonsteknologi (1+2)
Geofag (1+2)
Teknologi og forskningslære (1+2)
De spesielle opptakskravene kan også dekkes med fag fra videregående opplæring før Kunnskapsløftet, eller på andre måter .
HMS0503 – Laboratoriesikkerhet

prerequisiteText:
PROF1015 – Profesjonsidentitet, læring og undervisning

eller

PPU3220 – PPU del 2 av 2: Vurdering og tilpasset opplæring
eller andre tilsvarende emner.
I tillegg kreves følgende disiplinære emner:
Minimum 50 av 60 studiepoeng i lektorprogrammets 60 studiepoengs emnegruppe biologi eller andre tilsvarende emner.

A general cell biology course with immunology, such as BIOS3700 – Cell Biology 2 , is strongly recommended. BIOS3700 uses The Cell (Alberts et al., 6th Edition) as textbook. The content of Chapters 23: Pathogens and Infection and Chapter 24: The Innate and Adaptive Immune System, is assumed as prior knowledge for students taking this course.

prerequisiteText: Opptak til et PhD program.

metadata.recommendedPrerequisiteText: BIOS3700 – Cellebiologi 2 BIOS4030 – Cellebiologiske arbeidsmetoder eller tilsvarende kunnskaper.

A general cell biology course with immunology, such as BIOS3700 – Cell Biology 2 , is strongly recommended. BIOS3700 uses The Cell (Alberts et al., 6th Edition) as textbook. The content of Chapters 23: Pathogens and Infection and Chapter 24: The Innate and Adaptive Immune System, is assumed as prior knowledge for students taking this course.
